name: F-35 Lightning II

material_details.Titanium: Airframe structural components — 15% of airframe by weight, bulkheads and wing spars
material_details.Gallium: GaN/GaAs in AESA radar (AN/APG-81), electronic warfare suite, 5G-class data links
material_details.Germanium: Infrared sensor windows for EOTS and DAS (6 apertures), night vision optics
material_details.Cobalt: F135 engine superalloys — turbine blades and vanes operating above 1,000C
material_details.Lithium: Onboard lithium-ion battery systems for backup power and APU start
material_details.Rare Earths: Permanent magnets in over 900 lbs of rare earth materials across actuators, guidance, and motors
material_details.Silicon: Semiconductor chips in avionics, mission computers, sensor fusion processors
material_details.Copper: Electrical wiring, power distribution, and signal cabling throughout airframe
material_details.Magnesium: Lightweight gearbox housings and transmission casings in F135 engine
material_details.Aluminum: Airframe skin panels, structural ribs, access panels, and internal framing
material_details.Nickel: F135 engine superalloy components — combustor liners, turbine disks
material_details.Graphite: Carbon fiber composite structures — wing skins, fuselage panels, stealth coatings
material_details.Beryllium: Sensor window frames, targeting pod structural components, lightweight brackets
material_details.Rhenium: Single-crystal turbine blade alloys in F135 — enables 1,150C operation
material_details.Niobium: High-temperature superalloy additive in F135 turbine components
material_details.Chromium: Stainless steel and superalloy content in engine and structural fasteners
material_details.Manganese: High-strength steel alloy content in landing gear and structural members
material_details.Tantalum: Capacitors in radar electronics, EW suite, and mission computers
material_details.Neodymium: NdFeB permanent magnets in electric actuators, flight control motors, and generators
material_details.Dysprosium: Heat-stabilized NdFeB magnets in fin actuators and high-temp motor applications
material_details.Samarium: SmCo magnets in radar gimbals and high-reliability actuators
material_details.Terbium: Co-additive with dysprosium for thermal stability in permanent magnets
material_details.Praseodymium: NdPr magnet alloy component in electric drive motors
material_details.Tungsten: Ballast weights, vibration damping, radiation shielding in sensor bays
material_details.Indium: Indium tin oxide (ITO) coatings on cockpit displays and sensor windows
material_details.Tin: Lead-free solder joints throughout avionics assemblies
material_details.Bismuth: Bismuth-based solder in temperature-sensitive electronics assemblies
material_details.Lead: Radiation shielding in select sensor compartments, legacy solder alloys
material_details.Zinc: Galvanized fasteners and zinc chromate corrosion protection coatings
material_details.Boron: Boron fiber composites in select structural reinforcements
material_details.Vanadium: Vanadium-alloyed titanium (Ti-6Al-4V) in primary airframe structure
material_details.Yttrium: Yttria-stabilized thermal barrier coatings on F135 turbine blades
material_details.Scandium: Scandium-aluminum alloys in lightweight structural castings

notes: Most complex weapons system ever built. 1,700+ suppliers. Contains over 900 lbs of rare earth materials per aircraft.

name: Virginia-class Submarine

material_details.Nickel: HY-80/HY-100 hull steel — primary pressure hull alloy
material_details.Chromium: Corrosion-resistant steel alloys for seawater-exposed components
material_details.Titanium: Seawater systems, sonar dome, non-magnetic fittings
material_details.Copper: Electrical systems — 200+ miles of cable per boat, motor windings
material_details.Rare Earths: Sonar transducers, electric motors, permanent magnet propulsion
material_details.Zinc: Sacrificial anodes for cathodic hull protection against seawater corrosion
material_details.Lead: Radiation shielding around S9G reactor compartment, ballast systems
material_details.Hafnium: Nuclear reactor control rods — absorbs neutrons without swelling
material_details.Zirconium: Nuclear fuel cladding in S9G reactor — transparent to neutrons
material_details.Silicon: Integrated circuits in combat systems, sonar processing, and navigation
material_details.Manganese: High-strength manganese steel in hull framing and structural members
material_details.Aluminum: Non-structural components, heat exchangers, lightweight internal fittings
material_details.Tin: Lead-free solder in electronics assemblies, bronze bearings (tin-copper alloy)
material_details.Bismuth: Bismuth-based solder in combat system electronics
material_details.Niobium: HSLA steel additive for hull and structural welds
material_details.Vanadium: Vanadium-alloyed hull steel for enhanced strength and toughness
material_details.Graphite: Nuclear reactor moderator material, seal and gasket compounds
material_details.Tantalum: Capacitors in sonar and combat system electronics
material_details.Uranium: Enriched uranium fuel for S9G naval reactor — 33-year core life
material_details.Silver: Electrical contacts, brazing alloys in reactor systems
material_details.Boron: Boron carbide neutron shielding around reactor compartment
material_details.Gadolinium: Neutron absorber in reactor shielding blankets
material_details.Neodymium: NdFeB magnets in permanent magnet propulsion motor
material_details.Dysprosium: Thermal stabilization of propulsion motor magnets

notes: Industrial base cannot currently meet 2/year rate. S9G reactor uses HEU fuel with 33-year core life.

material_details.Aluminum: Armor and structural components, wheel assemblies
material_details.Manganese: High-strength steel armor plate alloy content
material_details.Chromium: Armor steel alloy for ballistic protection

material_details.Lead: Lead-acid starter batteries, ballistic-grade glass laminates

name: M1A2 Abrams SEPv3
